definition: PBOC monetary policy pass-through efficiency is claimed to be ~30-40% of Fed equivalence, driven by NIM compression to 1.5-1.7% impairing the bank lending channel. However, PBOC's primary tools (window guidance, RRR, relending) operate outside the interest rate channel, making rate-based pass-through comparisons misleading.
evidence: NIM compression to 1.5-1.7% impairs bank lending channel
Specific 30-40% ratio is false precision per pair_3
Administered channels operate outside interest rate channel, making comparison misleading
open_questions: How should PBOC transmission be measured if rate-based comparisons are misleading?
Is NIM compression accelerating or stabilizing?
What are the relative efficacy weights of PBOC's non-rate tools?
